summary: African champions (AFCON 2023) and a dark horse under Emerse Faé, who took over mid-tournament — after a 4-0 group humbling by Equatorial Guinea — and led the 'more than a fairytale' run to the title. The only African side not to concede in 2026 qualifying, and top scorers (25). Chasing a first-ever World Cup knockout appearance.
style: 4-3-3 — short goal kicks with a trademark angled ball from the full-back into the No 9's feet; midfielders rotate to get on the ball and let the centre-backs spread wide. Threat is overwhelmingly down the wings, with a wealth of wide talent (Amad, Adingra, Pépé, Diomandé). At AFCON 2025 they ranked 3rd for possession and top for crosses, shots and dribbles per game.

counterReliance.note: Possession-and-wing-led on a disciplined base; can also break at pace

strengths: Elite defensive record — only African team not to concede in qualifying, while finishing top scorers (25)
Outstanding wide players who both create and finish
Consistent corner threat (all delivery types) — compensates for the lack of a recognised No 9
weaknesses: No settled first-choice No 9 (Guessand, Diakité, the switched-allegiance Wahi in contention)
Can overplay against a high press — turnover risk (Germany especially)
Mid-block defending: centre-backs mark touch-tight (Egypt exploited it, 3-2, to knock them out of AFCON 2025)

keyPlayers.name: Amad Diallo
keyPlayers.note: The poster boy — stays high and wide with the overlapping Doué; 3 goals at AFCON, excels 1v1, cultured left foot, arrives for rebounds

keyPlayers.name: Franck Kessié
keyPlayers.note: Ever-present under Faé; technical quality plus a reliable goal threat (6 in 29 caps); closing on Drogba's cap record

tactics: Build patiently, work the wings, and lean on relentless wide quality and corner delivery rather than a fixed striker. Disciplined and hard to beat, but vulnerable in a mid-block to teams that pin the centre-backs — and to turnovers if they overplay versus Germany's press.
notes: Faé kept the 4-3-3 he adopted on taking charge during the title run; fourth World Cup, still seeking a first knockout berth.
sources: The Athletic — 2026 World Cup tactical guide, Group E (5 Jun 2026)
John Walker (@johnwalker_1986) — pre-tournament tactical scouting, May–Jun 2026
